rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing internal illness (šà-gig) caused by spell (na)
Therapeutic or protective: scorpion's tail as remedy for impotence or as physical threat

rec.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 340-341, tav. XXXVII [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla l, 21-23 [1984]
rec.func: Therapeutic or protective: scorpion's tail as remedy for impotence or as physical threat

rec.pub: Matthiae, P., Ebla: Un impero inciso nell’argilla (2nd ed.), tav. 64 (obv.) [1979]; Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 342-343, tav. XXXVIII [1979]; Matthiae, P., Ebla: Un impero inciso nell’argilla,, tav. 47 (obv.) [1979]; Mander, P., Or. 48, 335 n. 2, 336-339 [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 1.24 [1984]; Matthiae, P., I tesori di Ebla, tav. 27c (obv.) [1984]
rec.func: Therapeutic or protective: scorpion's tail as remedy for impotence or as physical threat
Possibly therapeutic: releasing spell (na) in Text 9a, incantation (mu -- syllabic writing for mu7) in Text 9b
Possibly therapeutic: releasing spell (na) in Text 9a, incantation (mu -- syllabic writing for mu7) in Text 9b
Protective: protecting against snakes and scorpions
Protective: protecting against snakes and scorpions

rec.gods: NE.DAG, father (of the deities) and his offspring
rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing internal illness (šà-gig) possibly caused by incantation (mu7/tu6)
Therapeutic: releasing eye-ache (igi-gig) caused by Enki
Possibly therapeutic: similar to therapeutic Texts 30 and 31
rec.feat: Features praise of tamarisk as divine purifier

rec.pub: CIRPL Urn 49
rec.func: Temple-foundation: opening to royal inscription recording foundation of temple; more specifically, possibly used for opening the mouths of divine statues
rec.feat: Features praise of reed as divine purifier

rec.pub: Alberti, OrNS 50, p. 257 [1981]
Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 347-350, tav. XLI-XLII [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 10, 24, 25, 27-37 [1984]

rec.mre: See Zimmern 1918-19 p55 and Krebernik 1984 p198

rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness(-inducing) spell (na-gi -- syllabic writing for gig) possibly associated with Enki

rec.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 343, tav. XXXVIII [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 25 [1984]
rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness(-inducing) spell (na-gi -- syllabic writing for gig) possibly associated with Enki

rec.pub: Matthiae, P., Ebla: Un impero inciso nell’argilla (2nd ed.), tav. 64 (obv.) [1979]; Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 342-343, tav. rec.gods: Enki, Šamaš (both in an unclear context)

rec.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 347-350, tav. XLI-XLII [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 10, 24, 25, 27-37 [1984]

rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness (gi -- syllabic writing for gig)

rec.pub: Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 23, 38 [1984]
rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness (gi -- syllabic writing for gig)

rec.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 346, tav. XL [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla .9 [1984]; Matthiae, P., I tesori di Ebla, tav. 30 b- c [1984]
rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing internal illness (ša-gi -- syllabic writing for šà-gig), illness (dur -- syllabic writing for tur5) and bile (zé), caused by spell (na) and incantation (du -- syllabic writing for tu6)

rec.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 339, tav. XXXVI [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 19 [1984]
rec.gods: An, Enki and Ninki, Enlil, Utu
rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness (gig) possibly caused by incantation (du -- syllabic writing for tu6)
rec.feat: Features praise of tamarisk as divine purifier, divine oath and oath by heaven and the underworld

rec.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness (ki-gig and dúr -- syllabic writing for tur5)

rec.feat: Features praise of tamarisk as divine purifier, divine oath and oath by heaven and the underworld

rec.func: Possibly therapeutic: similar to later incantation releasing illness caused by the evil udug

rec.func: Therapeutic: references to neck problem (gú-kéš), the evil ala and possibly the udug
